title: Debug hanging server in container
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Running a server with auto-reload hangs in container. I use podman to run Flask with all dependencies in debug mode. This mode shows errors and provides auto-reload. And it hangs on start.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
git clone https://github.com/chubin/cheat.sh.git
cd cheat.sh
git checkout -b hanging 0df9db4

strace podman-compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.debug.yml up
```
Try to open http://127.0.0.1:8002/

**Describe the results you received:**

That's `strace` output, and that FUTEX messages at the end are repeating.
```
mmap(NULL, 8392704, PROT_NONE, MAP_PRIVATE|MAP_ANONYMOUS|MAP_STACK, -1, 0) = 0x7f2d1b7ff000
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Serving a request with GET status 200.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Oct  7 19:21:20 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Can you get a simpler repeater that does not use podman-compose?

This is what I see in the container.
```
 * Serving Flask app "bin/srv.py" (lazy loading)
 * Environment: development
 * Debug mode: on
 * Running on http://0.0.0.0:8002/ (Press CTRL+C to quit)
 * Restarting with stat
 * Debugger is active!
 * Debugger PIN: 475-112-999
```
I would figure your application is having an issue running within the container.  Have you tried this with a rootfull container?

@rhatdan do you mean `sudo podman run ...`? That hangs too.

The application contains a reloader. If I turn off reloader, it starts to work.
```
$ podman run -p 8002 -e FLASK_RUN_RELOAD=False yakshaveinc/linux:podman-8257
 * Serving Flask app "bin/srv.py"
 * Environment: development
 * Debug mode: on
^CKeyboardInterrupt
2020-11-13T00:23:47Z
```
Does it work if you run it --privileged?

This hangs too.
```
$ podman run --privileged -p 8002 yakshaveinc/linux:podman-8257
```

Then the issue is your process is having a hard time running inside of a container.

If the process inside of the container does not work in --privilege mode, then you can figure it is having a problem with container namespaces.  You can start disabling some of these also.

podman run --net=host --ipc=host --upc=host --pid=host ...

These will cause your container process to not use some of the namespaces.

Have you tried running your container as root also?

@rhatdan using `sudo` + `--privileged` works..
```
sudo podman run --privileged -p 8002 -it yakshaveinc/linux:podman-8257
```
These didn't work.
```
podman run  -p 8002 -it yakshaveinc/linux:podman-8257
sudo podman run  -p 8002 -it yakshaveinc/linux:podman-8257
podman run --privileged -p 8002 -it yakshaveinc/linux:podman-8257
```

> podman run --net=host --ipc=host --upc=host --pid=host ...
```
Error: unknown flag: --upc
➜  ~ podman run --help | grep -i Names
      --cgroupns string                          cgroup namespace to use
      --gidmap strings                           GID map to use for the user namespace
      --ipc string                               IPC namespace to use
      --pid string                               PID namespace to use
      --subgidname string                        Name of range listed in /etc/subgid for use in user namespace
      --subuidname string                        Name of range listed in /etc/subuid for use in user namespace
      --uidmap strings                           UID map to use for the user namespace
      --userns string                            User namespace to use
      --uts string                               UTS namespace to use
```

Setting `--uts host` helped. Although I have no idea why.
```
➜  ~ podman run --uts=host -p 8009:8002 -it yakshaveinc/linux:podman-8257
 * Serving Flask app "bin/srv.py" (lazy loading)
 * Environment: development
 * Debug mode: on
 * Running on http://0.0.0.0:8002/ (Press CTRL+C to quit)
 * Restarting with stat
/app/bin/srv.py:27: MonkeyPatchWarning: Monkey-patching outside the main native thread. Some APIs will not be available. Expect a KeyError to be printed at shutdown.
  patch_all()
/app/bin/srv.py:27: MonkeyPatchWarning: Monkey-patching not on the main thread; threading.main_thread().join() will hang from a greenlet
  patch_all()
 * Debugger is active!
 * Debugger PIN: 128-090-104
/app/bin/srv.py:27: MonkeyPatchWarning: Monkey-patching outside the main native thread. Some APIs will not be available. Expect a KeyError to be printed at shutdown.
  patch_all()
/app/bin/srv.py:27: MonkeyPatchWarning: Monkey-patching not on the main thread; threading.main_thread().join() will hang from a greenlet
  patch_all()
127.0.0.1 - - [17/Nov/2020 02:34:05] "GET / HTTP/1.1" 500 -
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/flask/_compat.py", line 39, in reraise
    raise value
  File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 991, in _find_and_load
    
  File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 152, in __exit__
    
  File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 107, in release
    
RuntimeError: cannot release un-acquired lock
127.0.0.1 - - [17/Nov/2020 02:34:05] "GET /?__debugger__=yes&cmd=resource&f=style.css HTTP/1.1" 200 -
127.0.0.1 - - [17/Nov/2020 02:34:05] "GET /?__debugger__=yes&cmd=resource&f=jquery.js HTTP/1.1" 200 -
127.0.0.1 - - [17/Nov/2020 02:34:05] "GET /?__debugger__=yes&cmd=resource&f=debugger.js HTTP/1.1" 200 -
...
```

On the second try it didn't.
```
➜  ~ podman run --uts=host -p 8020:8002 -it yakshaveinc/linux:podman-8257
 * Serving Flask app "bin/srv.py" (lazy loading)
 * Environment: development
 * Debug mode: on
 * Running on http://0.0.0.0:8002/ (Press CTRL+C to quit)
 * Restarting with stat
/app/bin/srv.py:27: MonkeyPatchWarning: Monkey-patching outside the main native thread. Some APIs will not be available. Expect a KeyError to be printed at shutdown.
  patch_all()
/app/bin/srv.py:27: MonkeyPatchWarning: Monkey-patching not on the main thread; threading.main_thread().join() will hang from a greenlet
  patch_all()
 * Debugger is active!
 * Debugger PIN: 412-679-934
/app/bin/srv.py:27: MonkeyPatchWarning: Monkey-patching outside the main native thread. Some APIs will not be available. Expect a KeyError to be printed at shutdown.
  patch_all()
/app/bin/srv.py:27: MonkeyPatchWarning: Monkey-patching not on the main thread; threading.main_thread().join() will hang from a greenlet
  patch_all()
```

There are a plenty of bugs. Firefox hangs more often that `curl`. Maybe because FF sends keep-alive by default. I don't know.
```
    
  File "src/gevent/libev/corecext.pyx", line 1209, in gevent.libev.corecext.child.__cinit__
    
TypeError: child watchers are only available on the default loop
```

This might be a Firefox issue.. Another "interesting" log excerpt.
```
...
init__
    
TypeError: child watchers are only available on the default loop
10.88.0.1 - - [17/Nov/2020 03:16:58] code 400, message Bad request version ('í¬ñ\x00"\x13\x01\x13\x03\x13\x02À+À/Ì©Ì¨À,À0À')

10.88.0.1 - - [17/Nov/2020 03:16:58] "üý¯¯ýkç].Wßßñ�ùaNõKÚ"
' eZ¥¡1µ"À+À/Ì©Ì¨À,À0À" HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST -            Wµ²=Í_Ê]DRShÅóJÛº

```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Ok I will blame this on firefox. closing.

Firefox and `curl` are treated differently in app. The cause is most likely some deadlock happening between watcher event loop and `gevent` patches event loop. I thought that maybe `podman` employs some best practices in debugging those issues, like `strace` flags etc.

As a workaround I switched Flask app to use `flask run` server with reload watcher instead of `gevent`.
